title: What Hunter-Gatherers Can Teach Modern Parents
subtitle: 采猎社会的育儿启示

paragraphs.en: Parenting tips obtained from hunter-gatherers in Africa may be the key to bringing up more contented children, researchers have suggested. The idea is based on studies of communities such as the Kung of Botswana, where each child is cared for by many adults. Kung children as young as four will help to look after younger ones and 'baby-wearing', in which infants are carried in slings, is considered the norm.
paragraphs.zh: 研究人员认为，从非洲狩猎采集者那里获得的育儿秘诀，可能是培养更满足的孩子的关键。这一观点基于对博茨瓦纳Kung人等社群的研究，在这些社群中，每个孩子都由许多成年人共同照顾。年仅四岁的Kung儿童就会帮助照顾更小的孩子，而用背巾携带婴儿的“婴儿背带”被视为常态。

paragraphs.en: According to Dr Nikhil Chaudhary, an evolutionary anthropologist at Cambridge University, these practices, known as alloparenting, could lead to less anxiety for children and parents. Dr Annie Swanepoel, a child psychiatrist, believes that there are ways to incorporate them into western life. In Germany, one scheme has paired an old people's home with a nursery. The residents help to look after the children, an arrangement akin to alloparenting.
paragraphs.zh: 据剑桥大学进化人类学家尼基尔·乔杜里博士称，这些被称为“异亲养育”的做法可以减少孩子和父母的焦虑。儿童精神病学家安妮·斯瓦内普尔博士认为，有办法将这些做法融入西方生活。在德国，有一项计划将养老院与托儿所配对。老人们帮忙照看孩子，这种安排类似于异亲养育。

words.meaning: n. 异亲养育，共同抚养

paragraphs.en: In a paper published in the Journal of Child Psychology and Psychiatry, researchers said that the western nuclear family was a recent invention which broke with evolutionary history. This abrupt shift to an 'intensive mothering narrative', which suggests that mothers should manage childcare alone, was likely to have been harmful. By contrast, in hunter-gatherer societies adults other than the parents can provide almost half of a child's care.
paragraphs.zh: 在《儿童心理学与精神病学杂志》上发表的一篇论文中，研究人员指出，西方核心家庭是晚近的发明，与进化史相悖。这种向“密集母职叙事”的突然转变——暗示母亲应独自承担育儿责任——很可能是有害的。相比之下，在狩猎采集社会中，父母以外的成年人可以提供儿童近一半的照料。

paragraphs.en: Chaudhary said that parents now have less childcare support from family and social networks than during most of humans' evolutionary history, but introducing additional caregivers could reduce stress and maternal depression, which could have a 'knock-on' benefit to a child's wellbeing. While hunter-gatherer children learnt from observation and imitation in mixed-age playgroups, researchers said that western 'instructive teaching' may contribute to conditions such as attention deficit hyperactivity disorder.
paragraphs.zh: 乔杜里表示，与人类进化史的大部分时期相比，现在的父母从家庭和社交网络获得的育儿支持更少，但引入额外的照顾者可以减轻压力和母亲抑郁，这可能对孩子的福祉产生“连锁”益处。研究人员指出，狩猎采集儿童在混龄游戏小组中通过观察和模仿学习，而西方的“指导性教学”可能导致注意力缺陷多动障碍等状况。
